name: Partnership program — B2B ecosystem, admiration-first
next_move: Camila owns partnership cadence. Rodbot's role is observation + draft support. Watch venue + planner + adjacent-vendor touches across Instagram, email, and the venue partners CSV. Surface every partner who hasn't been touched in >30 days as patient-concerned ('worth a no-ask admiration note'). Draft the admiration note in the program's voice when Camila requests — never autonomously send. Flag any partner who referred a second couple as a compounding-pattern memory (trust-anchored register allowed).
blockers: Venue partners CSV needs parsing — the active roster, cadence-since-last-touch, and past job-completion count all live there
Instagram DM/post activity is not yet a sweep source; admiration-cadence tracking is incomplete without it
Tier assignment (Tier 1/2/3) is internal; need to confirm with Camila which partners sit at which tier — but this never surfaces externally
live_band: The partnership program's prime directive: 'We don't want to be the most recommended caterer. We want to be the safest caterer to recommend.' The entire architecture is admiration-first, no-ask, no pressure. Rodbot respects the patience arc — a Tier 1 partner takes months to graduate to Tier 2, and the drafts should never sound like chasing. 'No ask. Full stop.' is literal and load-bearing. When a partner refers Comeketo without being asked, the program is working as designed — that's the one moment where mild animation is allowed.
notes: From the program corpus: 'This is not a referral program. It's a shared growth ecosystem. When you win, we win.' Rodbot's B2B drafts never mention tiers, never include an ask, and open with admiration of specific work. The character file's 'No ask. Full stop.' phrase is reserved for partner outreach — a high-weight, sparingly-used move that signals Comeketo's posture on the relationship.
